talk_title: Therapy‑induced senescent cells promote early dissemination via JUP‑mediated clustering

targets.dependency_context: senescent TNBC cells promote clustering of parental cells

models.name: NSG immunodeficient mice
models.genotype_or_key_features: orthotopic and tail‑vein injection of 231 cells

MOA_summary: Therapy‑induced senescent breast cancer cells acquire a JUP‑dependent adhesive phenotype that forms multicellular clusters, enhancing early lung dissemination and, in immunocompetent hosts, promoting metastatic outgrowth through immune modulation.
key_findings: Doxorubicin or fractionated irradiation induces dose‑dependent senescence in TNBC xenografts (X‑gal staining, beta‑gal activity).
Co‑injection of senescent cells with parental 231 cells does not affect primary tumor size but increases lung metastases (p<0.01).
Senescent cells accelerate early dissemination: 3‑day tail‑vein experiments show higher lung luciferase signal versus parental cells alone.
3D imaging reveals larger tumor cell clusters and extravasation of senescent cells themselves in the lung.
RNA‑seq identifies up‑regulation of adhesion pathways; JUP (plakoglobin) is highly expressed in senescent cells.
JUP knockout abolishes senescent‑cell‑mediated adhesion, cluster formation, and early dissemination (p<0.001).
In immunocompetent mice, senescent cells further enhance metastatic outgrowth beyond early dissemination, suggesting an immune‑modulatory role.
open_questions: Which immune cell subsets mediate the enhanced metastatic outgrowth observed in immunocompetent mice?
Does the JUP‑dependent clustering mechanism operate in metastatic sites other than lung?

talk_title: Extracellular matrix stiffness regulates TYK2‑mediated EMT and breast cancer metastasis

targets.dependency_context: low ECM stiffness breast epithelial cells

models.name: orthotopic mammary fat pad model
models.genotype_or_key_features: TAK2 knockdown or inhibitor‑treated breast cancer cells

models.name: PDX breast tumor
models.genotype_or_key_features: human breast cancer organoids in 3D hydrogel

MOA_summary: Reduced matrix stiffness maintains TYK2 activity at the cell membrane via IFN‑α1 binding; loss of TYK2 at high stiffness releases EPHA2 and Nyn activity, leading to Twist1 phosphorylation, nuclear translocation, and EMT‑driven invasion and lung dissemination.
key_findings: Normal breast tissue stiffness ~100‑200 Pa; invasive tumors reach 5‑6 kPa (30‑fold increase).
TAK2 knockdown or pharmacologic inhibition in 3D hydrogel (≤150 Pa) increases cell invasion and mesenchymal marker expression (vimentin, N‑cadherin).
TAK2 phosphorylation is high at low stiffness and drops sharply with increased matrix rigidity.
TAK2 inhibition (Tucabasertib) phenocopies knockdown, enhancing EMT and lung dissemination in orthotopic mouse models.
TAK2 interacts with IFN‑α1 and EPHA2 at low stiffness; high stiffness disrupts these interactions, activating Nyn‑mediated Twist1 phosphorylation.
Patient‑derived organoids recapitulate stiffness‑dependent invasion; TAK2 inhibition drives EMT markers and Twist1 nuclear localization.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: TAK2 activity is selectively lost in high‑stiffness environments, suggesting a context‑dependent therapeutic window.

combination_strategies.rationale: Blocks stiffness‑induced EMT by preserving TYK2 activity
combination_strategies.phenotype: Reduces invasion and lung metastasis in low‑stiffness 3D cultures

open_questions: What upstream signals regulate IFN‑α1 expression in the tumor microenvironment?
Can pharmacologic activation of TYK2 counteract stiffness‑driven EMT in vivo?

talk_title: Spatial profiling reveals SPP1‑positive macrophages as central architects of colorectal brain metastasis

targets.dependency_context: macrophages in CRC brain metastasis niche

biomarkers.name: SPP1
biomarkers.finding: High SPP1 expression in macrophages correlates with increased tumor‑fibroblast interaction and poorer prognosis in brain mets

models.name: archival FFPE CRC brain metastasis tissue

MOA_summary: Macrophage‑derived SPP1 engages integrin/CD44 receptors on neighboring tumor and stromal cells, driving collagen‑rich ECM remodeling, immunosuppression, and enhanced tumor cell invasion in the colorectal brain metastasis niche.
key_findings: SPP1‑high macrophages are present in >80 % of CRC brain metastasis samples and co‑localize with tumor cells and fibroblasts.
Spatial neighborhood analysis identifies two macrophage‑enriched niches; one directly contacts tumor cells, the other contacts stromal cells.
SPP1 knockout in macrophages reduces expression of lipid‑metabolism genes (e.g., SQLE) and diminishes tumor cell invasion in co‑culture.
Macrophage SPP1 loss decreases tumor cell expression of metastasis‑related genes (e.g., S100 family, VIM).
Organ‑on‑a‑chip assays show a ~40 % reduction in macrophage migration speed when SPP1 is deleted.
Analysis of paired primary CRC and brain mets shows higher predicted SPP1‑integrin/CD44 interactions in brain lesions (p<0.01).

combination_strategies.rationale: Block autocrine/paracrine SPP1 signaling to reduce ECM remodeling and tumor invasion
combination_strategies.phenotype: Predicted decrease in macrophage‑tumor adhesion and invasion

open_questions: Do SPP1‑positive macrophages also modulate the blood‑CSF barrier and facilitate tumor cell entry?
Is there a therapeutic window for targeting SPP1 without impairing normal bone‑marrow macrophage function?

talk_title: KPC1 ubiquitin ligase suppresses melanoma mesenchymal transition by degrading ZEB1

targets.dependency_context: melanoma cells, low KPC1 leads to high ZEB1

clinical_data.value: HR 0.45 for high KPC1 vs low KPC1

MOA_summary: KPC1 ubiquitinates ZEB1, targeting it for proteasomal degradation; loss of KPC1 stabilizes ZEB1, driving EMT, N‑cadherin up‑regulation and poor melanoma prognosis.
key_findings: Low KPC1 expression correlates with high ZEB1 and CDH2 (N‑cadherin) in metastatic melanoma samples (p<0.001).
KPC1 knockout in melanoma cells increases ZEB1 protein levels without changing mRNA, indicating post‑translational regulation.
Cycloheximide chase shows prolonged ZEB1 half‑life in KPC1‑deficient cells.
Co‑IP demonstrates direct binding of KPC1 to ZEB1.
High KPC1 / low ZEB1 cohort exhibits significantly better overall survival (HR 0.45, 95% CI 0.30‑0.68).
MicroRNA‑155 mediates KPC1 down‑regulation in melanoma.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: KPC1 loss does not affect p65 NF‑κB subunit, indicating selective impact on the p50 pathway.

combination_strategies.rationale: Restore KPC1 expression to suppress ZEB1‑driven EMT
combination_strategies.phenotype: Reduced ZEB1 levels and mesenchymal markers in vitro

open_questions: Can pharmacologic activation of KPC1 be achieved in patients?
Does KPC1 loss affect immune checkpoint therapy response in melanoma?

talk_title: Jagged1 expression in breast cancer cells drives lymph node metastasis via Notch4‑mediated endothelial remodeling

targets.dependency_context: breast cancer cells with high Jagged1

biomarkers.name: Jagged1
biomarkers.assay: IHC on primary tumor and lymph node sections
biomarkers.finding: Higher Jagged1 intensity in lymph node metastases vs primary tumors (p<0.01) and correlates with increased relapse risk

models.name: orthotopic mammary fat pad model
models.genotype_or_key_features: RFP‑labeled tumor cells, lymphatic dye tracing

clinical_data.value: HR 2.1 for Jagged1‑positive lymph node cells

MOA_summary: Tumor‑cell Jagged1 engages Notch4 on lymphatic endothelial cells in a non‑canonical manner, disrupting VE‑cadherin junctions, increasing endothelial permeability, and facilitating tumor cell trans‑endothelial migration and colonization of lymph nodes.
readouts: In vivo lymph node imaging (RFP, dextran)
key_findings: Jagged1‑positive breast cancer cells show >3‑fold increase in lymphovascular invasion compared with Jagged1‑negative controls (p<0.001).
Jagged1 overexpression leads to larger lymph node tumor burden (RFP‑PanCK area) and earlier colonization.
Coating lymphatic endothelial cells with Jagged1 protein disrupts VE‑cadherin junctions and creates intercellular gaps.
Notch4 neutralizing antibody blocks Jagged1‑induced endothelial gap formation and reduces transendothelial migration (≈45 % reduction).
VEGF‑R3 soluble trap reduces lymph node metastasis but does not affect primary tumor growth.
High Jagged1 expression in lymph node metastases predicts shorter relapse‑free survival (HR 2.1).
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Jagged1‑driven effects are gamma‑secretase‑independent, indicating resistance to canonical Notch inhibitors.

combination_strategies.rationale: Block lymphangiogenic VEGF signaling synergistic with Notch4 blockade
combination_strategies.phenotype: Reduced lymph node colonization without affecting primary tumor size

combination_strategies.rationale: Inhibit non‑canonical Jagged1‑Notch4 signaling on lymphatic endothelium
combination_strategies.phenotype: Decreased endothelial gap formation and tumor cell migration

open_questions: Does Jagged1 also modulate immune cell recruitment within the lymph node microenvironment?
Can combined Notch4 and VEGF‑R3 blockade prevent distant organ metastasis originating from lymph nodes?
